_comment: Deterministic output — formatting is exact given the input.

formatted_messages.subject: Research Insight [ACTION REQUIRED]: Recurring tasks: the hidden retention driver
formatted_messages.body:
Source: Q3 2026 Research Synthesis | Confidence: HIGH | ACTION REQUIRED

CORE INSIGHT: Recurring tasks is used by only 28% of accounts but drives 2x NPS and 3x retention for those accounts. Critically, usability testing shows 60% of new users fail to find the feature during their first session because it is buried in the date picker with no visual affordance.

IMPLICATION: Making recurring tasks more discoverable in onboarding could be the single highest-ROI product change this quarter.

FOR ENGINEERING:
ACTION: Move recurring task button to task header — not inside date picker. Estimated effort: 2 sprints. Directly tied to 3x retention signal.

formatted_messages.subject: Research Insight [ACTION REQUIRED]: Recurring tasks: the hidden retention driver
formatted_messages.body:
Source: Q3 2026 Research Synthesis | Confidence: HIGH | ACTION REQUIRED

CORE INSIGHT: Recurring tasks is used by only 28% of accounts but drives 2x NPS and 3x retention for those accounts. Critically, usability testing shows 60% of new users fail to find the feature during their first session because it is buried in the date picker with no visual affordance.

IMPLICATION: Making recurring tasks more discoverable in onboarding could be the single highest-ROI product change this quarter.

FOR DESIGN:
ACTION: Redesign recurring task entry point. Current: buried in date picker. Proposed: prominent labelled button in task header with clock icon. See usability test report for participant failure clips.

CORE INSIGHT: Recurring tasks is used by only 28% of accounts but drives 2x NPS and 3x retention for those accounts. Critically, usability testing shows 60% of new users fail to find the feature during their first session because it is buried in the date picker with no visual affordance.

FOR COMPANY-ALL-HANDS:
INSIGHT: We have a feature that drives 3x customer retention but only 28% of customers use it — because they can't find it. Our top priority this quarter is making it discoverable. That's it. That's the insight.

CORE INSIGHT: Recurring tasks is used by only 28% of accounts but drives 2x NPS and 3x retention for those accounts. Critically, usability testing shows 60% of new users fail to find the feature during their first session because it is buried in the date picker with no visual affordance.

FOR SALES:
SALES TALKING POINT: Recurring tasks (one of our core features) saves customers 2+ hours per week. In Q3, we are making it more visible in the product so you can demo the value on first contact. Talking point: 'ProjectFlow's recurring tasks saves your team 2 hours per week on average — and it takes 30 seconds to set up.'
